problem_statement: After a brief queue-worker outage, some municipal permits show a passed final inspection but never receive a certificate. Replaying delivery at the broker appears successful and no records are corrupt. Determine the earliest corrective target from immutable event history, audit rows, and traces without changing permit records.

observations.statement: All 37 affected permits have a final-inspection event, a processed-event marker, no certificate row, and no certificate-render request.
The affected event deliveries were attempted twice: the first worker vanished 9 to 31 milliseconds after inserting the marker, and the second delivery returned duplicate without requesting a render.
observations.provenance: broker delivery ledger joined to worker spans

observations.statement: For unaffected permits on the same build, the marker insert and render-request insert share one transaction identifier; for affected permits the marker transaction committed alone before the connection ended.
The renderer, template store, and document sink processed unrelated requests throughout the outage window, and no failed request exists for any affected permit.
observations.provenance: downstream service counters and sink ledger

observations.statement: Source revision, event schema, worker settings, database engine revision, and certificate template hash match the prior healthy day.
The worker-loss interval aligns with an orchestrator network partition, but permits whose first attempt ended before marker commit were redelivered and completed normally.
observations.provenance: orchestrator journal and permit outcome join

observations.statement: A read-only state-machine reconstruction finds no permitted transition from marked-without-request to certificate-issued because every later delivery exits at the marker check.
observations.provenance: offline transition replay from immutable audit events

observations.statement: A sealed fault-injection proof that places the marker and render request in one atomic unit leaves neither row after loss and completes both on redelivery; ordinary duplicate deliveries still emit one request.
observations.provenance: isolated database and broker proof transcript

observations.statement: The certificate absence is thus upstream of rendering and storage alarms: those systems never receive a causally linked request for an affected permit.
